title: telanthera porrigens
intro: Telanthera porrigens, also known as the "Sensitive Plant" or "Touch-Me-Not," is a perennial plant native to tropical regions of the Americas, characterized by its compound leaves that fold up when touched or disturbed. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Telanthera porrigens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the leaves typically being harvested and dried for use in herbal remedies.
